differential_diagnoses: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus with catastrophic antiphospholipid syndrome
Disseminated malignancy with nonbacterial thrombotic endocarditis
Primary angiitis of the central nervous system
next_steps: Order antiphospholipid antibody panel (lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin, anti-beta-2-glycoprotein I)
Perform transesophageal echocardiography to evaluate for valvular vegetations
Send serum and CSF for specialized infectious disease testing (Rickettsia, Leptospira, fungal markers)
Consult rheumatology for consideration of high-dose corticosteroids or intravenous immunoglobulin
Hold anticoagulation until intracranial hemorrhage is ruled out or stabilized
Serial neurologic assessment and monitoring of coagulation profile

differential_diagnoses: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) with secondary vasculitis
Disseminated malignancy with nonbacterial thrombotic endocarditis (NBTE)
next_steps: Order antiphospholipid antibody panel (lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin, anti-beta-2-glycoprotein I)
Perform transthoracic echocardiography followed by transesophageal echocardiography to assess for valvular vegetations
Measure ADAMTS13 activity and perform peripheral blood smear review for schistocytes
Test for ANCA (MPO and PR3) and perform serial CBC/CMP/LDH/haptoglobin
Consult Rheumatology for evaluation of potential autoimmune vasculitis or SLE flares
Continue broad-spectrum antibiotics while awaiting specific cultures and molecular diagnostic results
Perform serial neurologic evaluations and repeat neuroimaging if clinical status worsens
Avoid systemic anticoagulation until intracranial hemorrhage stability is confirmed and etiology (e.g., mycotic aneurysm vs. autoimmune vasculitis) is clarified
